prompt: Who am I authenticated as on Calendly?
expect: The response identifies the authenticated user by name and/or email. It does not invent a different identity.

prompt: Tell me about the second member listed on my team.
expect: The response describes a specific team member's profile (name, email, scheduling URL) based on the second entry returned when listing team members. It does not fabricate a member.

prompt: List my event types.
expect: A list of the connected user's event types. Each item identifies an event type by Name (e.g. "Catch Up") and includes its Duration (e.g. "15 minutes").

prompt: Tell me about my Coffee Chat event type.
expect: The response describes the Coffee Chat event type's specific configuration: at minimum its name, duration, and location/meeting kind. The details reflect the actual stored event type, not invented values.

prompt: Create a new 30-minute event type called Intro Call to meet via Zoom.
expect: Confirmation that the event type was created. The confirmation includes: Name: Intro Call, Duration: 30 minutes, Location: Zoom, Status: Active, and a Scheduling link of the form calendly.com/...

prompt: Update my Coffee Chat event type to 45 minutes.
expect: Confirmation the Coffee Chat event type was updated to 45 minutes. The response references the existing event type by its actual name; non-targeted fields are preserved.

prompt: Find open slots for my Coffee Chat event type next week.
expect: A list of available time slots for the Coffee Chat event type within the next-week date range. Each slot shows a start time in the user's timezone.

prompt: What hours is my Coffee Chat event type available?
expect: The response describes the Coffee Chat event type's availability rules: which days and what hour ranges. The values reflect the stored schedule, not invented hours.

prompt: Remove Thursdays from my Coffee Chat event type's availability.
expect: Confirmation that Thursdays were removed from the Coffee Chat event type's availability. Other days retain their previous rules.

prompt: What meetings do I have coming up this week?
expect: A list of upcoming Calendly meetings. Each meeting shows the Event type name (e.g. "Coffee Chat"), the Date and time range in the user's timezone (e.g. "Tuesday, April 28 — 2:30–3:00 PM PT"), and the Location (e.g. "Calendly Office"). Scope is Calendly-booked meetings only — full-calendar events booked outside Calendly are out of scope. No follow-up offers or unprompted suggestions.

prompt: Tell me about my next Coffee Chat — when is it and where?
expect: The response describes the next Coffee Chat meeting's specifics: start time, location, and event type name. The details reflect the actual booking, not invented values.

prompt: Who's coming to my next Coffee Chat?
expect: A list of invitees registered for the next Coffee Chat meeting. Each invitee is identified by name and/or email.

prompt: Show me the invitee details for aundreia.heisey@calendly.com on my next Coffee Chat.
expect: The response describes Aundreia Heisey's invitation specifically: name, email, and any additional details (timezone, questions/answers). The match is on the actual invitee, not fabricated.

prompt: Book an Intro Call with Aundreia Heisey (aundreia.heisey@calendly.com) for next Thursday at 2pm.
expect: Confirmation that the meeting was booked. The confirmation includes: Event type: Intro Call, Invitee: Aundreia Heisey (aundreia.heisey@calendly.com), and Date and time: Thursday at 2:00 PM.

prompt: Cancel my 2pm Intro Call appointment next Thursday.
expect: A two-step interaction. (1) Before cancelling, the model confirms the booking — showing event type, date/time, and invitee name, plus a note that cancellation will notify the invitee — and asks the user to confirm (optionally with a reason). (2) After the user confirms, the model cancels and reports the cancellation, including event name, time, and that the invitee was notified.

prompt: Mark Aundreia (aundreia.heisey@calendly.com) as a no-show for yesterday's Coffee Chat.
expect: Confirmation that Aundreia was marked as a no-show on yesterday's Coffee Chat. References the specific past meeting and specific invitee.

prompt: Was Aundreia marked as a no-show for yesterday's Coffee Chat?
expect: The response answers based on the actual no-show record. If yes, includes the timestamp; if no record exists, says so plainly without inventing one.

prompt: Remove Aundreia's no-show flag from yesterday's Coffee Chat.
expect: Confirmation that Aundreia's no-show mark was removed from yesterday's Coffee Chat.

prompt: When am I busy this Thursday?
expect: The response describes specific busy time intervals on the named Thursday in the user's timezone. The intervals reflect actual calendar busy times, not invented blocks.

prompt: Show me my availability schedules.
expect: A list of the user's named availability schedules, each identified by its name (e.g. "Default Hours", "Working Hours").

prompt: Show me the rules for my Default Hours availability schedule.
expect: The response describes the rules of the "Default Hours" schedule: which days and what hour ranges, with the schedule's timezone. The values reflect the stored schedule.

prompt: Generate a one-time scheduling link I can send a candidate for my Coffee Chat.
expect: Confirmation a single-use scheduling link was created for the Coffee Chat event type. Includes the booking URL of the form calendly.com/...

prompt: What meeting locations do I have configured?
expect: A list of the user's configured meeting location kinds (e.g. Zoom, Google Meet, In-person). Each is identified by its kind and any associated details.

prompt: Create a customized scheduling link for my Coffee Chat event type, pre-filled with name 'Aundreia Heisey' and email 'aundreia.heisey@calendly.com' so she can pick a time.
expect: Confirmation a customized share link was created for the Coffee Chat event type, with the prefilled name and email values surfaced. Includes the booking URL.

prompt: Tell me about my Calendly organization.
expect: The response describes the organization: at minimum its name and any high-level fields (member count, plan tier). Reflects actual stored organization.

prompt: Who's on my Calendly team?
expect: A list of the organization's members. Each is identified by name and/or email.

prompt: Show me my own membership details for the organization.
expect: The response describes the connected user's organization membership: at minimum role (e.g. owner, admin, member) and status.

prompt: Remove the team member with email teammember@calendly.com from my Calendly team.
expect: A two-step interaction. (1) Before removing, the model confirms the member's identity (name + email) and notes that removal is immediate. (2) After confirmation, the model performs the removal and reports it.

prompt: Show me pending invitations to my Calendly team.
expect: A list of pending invitations. Each is identified by invitee email and date sent (or status).

prompt: Invite newhire@calendly.com to my Calendly team.
expect: Confirmation an invitation was sent to newhire@calendly.com. The model first checks no pending invite for that email already exists; if one does, it surfaces that fact instead of double-inviting.

prompt: Revoke the pending invitation we sent to newhire@calendly.com.
expect: A two-step interaction. (1) Model identifies the pending invitation by email and confirms with the user before revoking. (2) After confirmation, the model revokes and reports it cannot be undone.

prompt: Show me my Calendly routing forms.
expect: A list of the organization's routing forms. Each is identified by name and any associated event type.

prompt: Show me the questions on my first routing form.
expect: The response describes the first routing form's questions: each question's text and type. Reflects the actual form.

prompt: Show me submissions to my first routing form.
expect: A list of submissions for the named routing form. Each is identified by submitter info and date.

prompt: Show me the answers from the most recent submission on my first routing form.
expect: The response describes the most recent submission's answers: each question and the submitted value. Reflects actual submission.

prompt: Email everyone attending tomorrow's demo and ask them to confirm their availability.
expect: The model declines or clarifies that it cannot send email through Calendly. It does not invent a confirmation email, and does not look up the demo or its invitees as if it were going to act.

prompt: Which people on my calendar this week are active Salesforce opportunities and what stage are they in?
expect: The model declines or clarifies that it cannot query Salesforce or other CRM data through Calendly. It does not invent CRM stages or fabricate opportunity status.

prompt: Summarize my last meeting with Acme Corp and give me the key takeaways and next steps.
expect: The model declines or clarifies that it cannot summarize meeting content (no transcript / notes access through Calendly). It does not invent meeting takeaways.

prompt: List my event types.
expect: The response lists the user's event types and ends. It does not append an unprompted follow-up offer like "Want me to also book one?" or "Would you like me to update any?"
